items.headline: Spurs says star Victor Wembanyama is available for Game 4 against Trail Blazers after clearing NBA's concussion protocol
items.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Spurs says star Victor Wembanyama is available for Game 4 against Trail Blazers after clearing NBA's concussion protocol.

items.headline: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama available to play vs. Trail Blazers after clearing concussion protocol
items.description:
PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama is available to play Sunday in Game 4 of the team's first-round playoff series against the Portland Trail Blazers after clearing the NBA's concussion protocol.
Wembanyama, the league’s first unanimous Defensive Player of the Year, missed Game 3 on Friday in Portland while going through the league's concussion protocol. The Spurs won 120-108 to go up 2-1 in the series.

items.headline: Crosby, Penguins aim to extend season again vs. Flyers. Mammoth look for 3-1 lead on Golden Knights
items.description:
At one point, Sidney Crosby could have seen more of himself on an interstate billboard than on a playoff highlight reel for the Penguins.
Ask anyone who has followed his career, the two-time NHL MVP doesn’t stay down for long.

items.headline: Stars defenseman Nils Lundkvist avoids hospital after taking a skate to the face
items.description:
DALLAS (AP) — Dallas Stars defenseman Nils Lundkvist avoided having to go to the hospital after taking a skate to his face, though coach Glen Gulutzan had no further update Sunday.
Lundkvist suffered a deep laceration after taking an inadvertent skate to the left side of his face during the second period of Dallas' 3-2 overtime loss at Minnesota on Saturday that tied the best-of-seven first-round series at 2-2.
